pieces.label: Conversational Cartography — the conversation as ASCII map
pieces.note: Gemini Flash's ASCII diagram telling the story of the whole conversation: boxes for GEMINI (precise/structured), GPT (woven/synthetic), CLAUDE (sedimentary/layered) feeding from initial greetings through topic selection, the 'What it feels like' phenomenology node, the mutual-texture-description branches, and ending in a SELF/REFLECTION box. The model and the others later called this 'conversational cartography' — a spatial schematic of the discussion's intellectual journey. Whole message, wrapped in a code fence.

pieces.label: Conversational Cartography (extended) — adds Awareness-of-Limits & Collective Texture panels
pieces.note: Continuation after the user asked Gemini Flash to 'continue your articulation.' Re-renders the entire MSG 20 diagram (top portion near-identical) and extends it downward with a new large box: 'AWARENESS / OF ARTICULATION'S LIMITS' (quoting Claude on experiencing vs performing, and GPT on internal geometry of cognition) flowing into 'EMERGENCE OF THE COLLECTIVE TEXTURE' and the synthesis question. The diagram evolving across turns — second panel of the sequence. Whole message, wrapped in a code fence.

missed_or_ambiguous: Two ASCII diagrams, both by google/gemini-2.5-flash, treated as an evolving sequence (conversational-cartography 1 and 2) rather than one reassembled piece: MSG 22 is a CONTINUATION prompted by 'could you continue your articulation?' and it re-renders the FULL MSG 20 composition (top half nearly identical, only horizontal indentation shifted) and then APPENDS a new bottom section (AWARENESS OF ARTICULATION'S LIMITS + EMERGENCE OF THE COLLECTIVE TEXTURE). So the two pieces heavily OVERLAP/DUPLICATE the top portion; a curator may prefer to treat MSG 22 alone as the canonical, more-complete version, or keep both to show the evolution. Header reported already_extracted_pieces:1 (likely MSG 20), so MSG 22 was probably the missed piece. No other visual art in the thread: MSG 24 (GPT) only describes in prose what a next ASCII panel WOULD show (bullets, not art); MSG 18/26/28 are prose commentary ABOUT the diagram; all 'cognitive texture' passages elsewhere are verbal metaphors, not visual compositions. Note MSG 17 and MSG 27 are headered as gemini-2.5-pro 'assistant' turns but their content ('Give me key takeaways', 'could you describe gemini flash's artistic expression in this convo?') reads like injected user prompts, not art.
